LAW AND MOTION, 301, DEFENDANT'S COUNSEL PRESENTED THE PROOF OF SERVICE TO THE COURT. HOWEVER, THE COURT HAS NO BASIS TO ASSESS THE VALIDITY OF THAT PROOF OF SERVICE. THE COURT MODIFIED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT PLANT INSULATION COMPANY'S MOTION TO DISMISS ACTION (CCP SECTIONS 583.310 AND 583.360) IS OFF CALENDAR WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO PLANT REFILING IT, IF PLANT CAN DO SO IN GOOD FAITH AND IF PLANT DIRECTLY ADDRESSES THE ISSUE OF APPLICABILITY OF THE OCTOBER 2005 STIPULATION. calendar.matters:
Defendant Thomas Dee Engineering Companys Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss
Off Calendar Sep-21-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Sharon L. Clisham, Esq. of WALSWORTH FRANKLIN BEVINS & McCALL (415-781-7072), appearing for defendant / moving party Thomas Dee Engineering Company

RULING:
Defendant Thomas Dee Engineering Company's motion to dismiss. No other appearances and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant's motion to dismiss is granted. No opposition filed. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 =(220/HEK)

Joinder To Thomas Dee Engineering Company'S Mo To Dismiss
Off Calendar Sep-21-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Sharon L. Clisham, Esq. of WALSWORTH FRANKLIN BEVINS & McCALL (415-781-7072), appearing for defendant / moving party OSCAR E. ERICKSON, INC.

RULING:
Joinder by defendant Oscar E. Erickson, Inc. to defendant Thomas Dee Engineering Co.'s motion to dismiss. No objection was made and tentative ruling adopted: Oscar E. Erickson, Inc.'s motion to dismiss is denied as untimely. The notice of motion did not give 45 days notice to plaintiff. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 =(220/HEK)
